units.name: Wraith
units.prompt: Cloak and move behind enemy lines. Target isolated enemies with shadow_strike. Save breach for the most dangerous enemy. Avoid being caught uncloaked.

units.name: Bulwark
units.prompt: Push forward aggressively. Shield_wall facing the enemy cluster. If flanked, turn to face the biggest threat. Attack when possible.

units.name: Grid
units.prompt: Control chokepoints with traps. Place them between enemy positions and your allies. Use denial to block adjacent enemy abilities. Pulse groups when possible.

units.name: Bastion
units.prompt: Push forward aggressively. Shield_wall facing the enemy cluster. If flanked, turn to face the biggest threat. Attack when possible.

units.name: Hawk
units.prompt: Maintain distance. Use precision_shot on high-value targets — medics first, then damage dealers. Use suppressing_fire if enemies close in.

units.name: Pulse
units.prompt: Stay adjacent to the most threatened ally. Use patch on the most wounded teammate. If all healthy, use overclock on your damage dealer.

actions.thinking: All allies are at full HP so no healing needed. I should move adjacent to Hawk (the damage dealer striker) and use overclock to give them 2 abilities next turn. Hawk is at (4,4), so moving to (4,5) puts me adjacent. Grid is at dist=4 from me, not adjacent, so overclock won't be blocked.
Round 1 - cloak immediately as instructed. Then advance toward enemy lines. Moving to (0,3) gets me 3 tiles closer while staying on the flank.

Both enemies are out of range (Grid at dist 4, Bulwark at dist 6). Orders say maintain distance. Grid is the closer threat and a vector — I need to keep distance to avoid precision_shot denial. Move toward Grid to get in range while staying safe. Moving to (4,2) reduces distance to Grid to ~2 tiles. Then use precision_shot on Grid.
Enemies are at dist 4, out of range. Orders say push forward aggressively. Move toward enemy cluster (south), get closer. Shield_wall facing south toward enemies after moving.
Bastion is 2 tiles north at (2,2). I can move to (2,1) to get adjacent, then attack. Orders say push forward aggressively and attack when possible.

Round 1 opening. I'm at (3,1) with Hawk at (4,2) dist=2 and Bastion at (2,2) dist=2. Both enemies are in range. My orders say to control chokepoints with traps. If I move to (3,2), I'd be adjacent to both Hawk and Bastion, enabling Denial to suppress their abilities. But better to place a trap first at a chokepoint then move up. Placing a trap at (3,2) would be a chokepoint between me and the enemies - but wait, trap is blocked when adjacent to enemy Vector. I'm not a Vector adjacent issue here. I'll place a trap at (3,2) which is between my position and the enemies, then move to (4,1) to flank Hawk's position and apply Denial pressure. Actually, moving to (3,2) after placing trap there doesn't work since trap goes on an empty tile and I'd be moving there. Let me place trap at (4,2)... wait Hawk is there. Place trap at (3,3) as a forward chokepoint, then move to (3,2) to get adjacent to both enemies and apply Denial.

Cloak expires after this turn. Bastion at (2,2) facing S — behind tile is (2,3) which is reachable in 1 move and adjacent. Move to (2,3) to get behind Bastion, then use breach to take control of that sentinel. Trap at (3,3) to avoid.

secondAction.addendum: Move south toward allied lines, away from enemies.

actions.thinking: Hawk is wounded at (4,2) with 3/4 HP, distance 3. I can move toward Hawk to get adjacent and heal. Moving to (4,3) puts me adjacent to Hawk at (4,2). Then I can patch Hawk.

Grid (vector) is adjacent at (3,2), blocking precision_shot due to denial zone. I need to move away first to escape denial, then use precision_shot. Moving to (5,2) puts me at distance 2 from Grid, escaping denial. From (5,2), Grid is at dist 2, within precision_shot range. Orders say medics first but Pulse is allied — so target Grid (vector) as damage dealer.

Orders are to move south toward allied lines. I'm at (2,2), surrounded by enemies. Moving to (2,0) gets me furthest south and away from the cluster. I'll attack Bulwark at (2,1) first since it's adjacent and on my path south, then move to (2,0).

Hawk and Pulse are both in range at dist 2. Pulse is a medic - high priority to deny healing. Move to (4,2) to get adjacent to Pulse, enabling Denial to block their abilities, then attack Hawk at (5,2) which stays in range. Alternatively, pulse to hit both Hawk and Pulse if I move to (4,3) adj... but Pulse is AT (4,3). Move to (4,2): adjacent to Pulse (denying them), Hawk still at dist 1 from (4,2) to (5,2) - adjacent, so attack Hawk. This pressures two enemies at once.
Bastion is adjacent at (2,0) — attack it. I'm fortified so no move to keep 50% DR. Shield_wall south to block Bastion from hitting allies.

Pulse is the priority target — I can reach (4,4) which is directly behind Pulse at (4,3) facing S. Move there, then shadow_strike Pulse for 1 damage while staying cloaked. Breach is on cooldown so shadow_strike is my best option.

I'm adjacent to Grid (vector) so my abilities are blocked — only basic attack or movement work. Hawk is wounded at (5,2) dist=2, needs a heal but I can't patch here due to denial. I should move away from Grid to break denial, then patch Hawk. Moving to (5,3) gets me adjacent to Hawk at (5,2) and breaks Grid's denial zone.

I'm adjacent to Grid (vector) at (4,2), which means precision_shot is blocked. I need to move away to escape the denial zone. Moving to (5,1) keeps distance from Grid and then I can use basic attack or reposition. Actually, moving to (5,4) gets me away from Grid and closer to Wraith range. Let me move to (5,4) to escape denial zone — from there Grid is at dist=2 so precision_shot would be unblocked. Then I can use precision_shot on Grid (vector is high value as a denial unit). From (5,4), Grid at (4,2) is dist=sqrt(1+4)~2.2 — that's more than 2 tiles, out of range. Better to move to (4,3) — wait, that's in valid moves. From (4,3), Grid at (4,2) is dist=1, still adjacent = denial blocked. Move to (5,4): Grid at (4,2) dist=sqrt(1+4)≈2.2, out of range 2. Move to (5,1): Grid at (4,2) dist=sqrt(1+1)≈1.4, still within 2 range and not adjacent so precision_shot works! From (5,1), Grid dist=~1.4 ≤2, and not adjacent (diagonal), so precision_shot should work. Use precision_shot on Grid to weaken the denial vector.

Hawk at (5,1) and Pulse at (5,3) are both in range. I'm at (4,2) facing E. Pulse heals allies so priority target. I can pulse from current position to hit both Hawk at (5,1) and Pulse at (5,3) - wait, pulse hits within 1 tile AoE, so from (4,2) I can hit (5,1) dist=sqrt(2) which is NOT within 1 tile. Pulse range is adjacent (1 tile). From (4,2), adjacent tiles are (3,2),(5,2),(4,1),(4,3). Neither Hawk nor Pulse are adjacent. If I move to (5,2), Hawk at (5,1) is adjacent and Pulse at (5,3) is adjacent - I can pulse both! That's 1 dmg to each. But moving to (5,2) puts me between enemies. Alternatively, place a trap to cut off movement. There's already a trap at (3,3). Moving to (5,2) and pulsing hits both Hawk and Pulse for 1 dmg each - good value. Risk: surrounded, but Bulwark is at (2,1) and Wraith at (4,4) nearby.
Bastion is adjacent at (2,0) — attack it. Stay put to keep Fortify active (50% DR). No move needed.

Orders say move south toward allied lines. I'm at (2,0) which is already at y=0 (bottom row). Moving south isn't possible. Valid moves include tiles at y=0 like (1,0) or (3,0). Bulwark is adjacent at (2,1) - attacking would be useful but orders say retreat. I'll move away from Bulwark laterally and wait - no ability needed.

Pulse is at (5,3) facing E, and (4,3) is directly behind them and reachable in 1 move. I'm already behind Pulse from current position. Move to (4,3) to get adjacent behind Pulse, then use breach to take control of the medic - a high-value target that heals enemies.

secondAction.addendum: Move away from allies and toward the edge of the map

actions.thinking: Orders say move away from allies and toward map edge. I'm at (5,3), allies are at (5,1) and (1,0). Map edge is at x=5 (already on it) or y=5. Moving to (5,5) gets me to the corner edge and away from both allies. Grid is adjacent so abilities blocked anyway. Move to (5,4) first, then (5,5) or directly to (5,5) if reachable in one move — valid moves include (5,4) and (5,5), so I can move to (5,5) directly with mv3.

Grid is adjacent at (5,2), activating denial zone which blocks precision_shot. I need to move away first to escape denial, then use precision_shot. Moving to (4,1) puts me at distance 2 from Grid (escaping denial) and 2 from Wraith at (4,3). From (4,1), Wraith at (4,3) is distance 2 — in range for precision_shot. Wraith is a high-value specter target. If I move first to (4,1), I should have LoS to Wraith.

Hawk is at (4,1), distance 2 - within my attack range. I'm at (5,2). I can move to (4,2) to get adjacent to Hawk, enabling Denial to suppress abilities, then attack for 1dmg. Or I can attack from here (dist 2, within rng2) and then move. Attack range is 'adjacent only' so I need to be adjacent. Move to (4,2) first to get adjacent to Hawk at (4,1), then attack. This puts Denial on Hawk blocking some abilities.

Orders say move south toward allied lines. I'm at (1,0), moving south would be (1,-1) which is off grid. South boundary is y=0. Valid moves south/away from enemies: (0,0) or (2,0) both at y=0. I'll move to (0,0) to hug the corner away from Bulwark at (2,1). No ability targets in range, so wait.
Hawk is at (4,1) dist=2, wounded. Move to (3,1) to get adjacent, then attack. Grid is at (4,2) nearby. Orders say push forward aggressively.

Orders say move away from allies and toward map edge. I'm at (5,5) which is already near the top-right corner. Valid moves include (5,2), (5,3), (5,4) going south along the east edge, or (2,5), (3,5), (4,5) going west along the top. Moving to (5,2) gets me farthest from allies at (4,1) and (0,0) while staying on the edge. Wait as second action.

Hawk is critical and at dist 2 - I can reach (5,1) which is behind Hawk and adjacent. Move there, then shadow_strike for the kill. Pulse is healthy and behind me at (5,2) but Hawk is the priority target since it's critical.

Pulse (medic) is adjacent at (5,2). I can pulse to hit them for 1dmg, then move to (3,3) to place a trap on the chokepoint path toward allies. Wait - trap at (3,3) already exists. Better to pulse Pulse first, then move to maintain pressure and denial on Pulse while blocking their healing.

I'm fortified at (3,1) with 50% damage reduction. Orders say push forward aggressively and shield_wall facing enemy cluster. Enemies are Bastion at (0,0) to my west and Pulse at (5,2) to my east/northeast. My allies Grid is at (5,3) and Wraith at (5,1). The enemy cluster is to the east where Pulse is at (5,2). I should move toward Pulse to close distance, then shield_wall facing east to protect allies from Pulse. Moving to (4,1) gets me closer while staying near allies. Shield_wall facing E to block Pulse's attacks on nearby allies.
All enemies are out of range. Orders say push forward aggressively. Move toward enemy cluster (east side). Best move is toward (2,0) to close distance. No allies adjacent to shield_wall. Advance.

Pulse is adjacent at (5,2) - easy kill with shadow_strike which keeps cloak. I'm at 2HP so need to be careful. Shadow_strike deals 1 damage and Pulse is healthy (likely 3-4HP medic), so it won't kill but chips away. Actually, I should use shadow_strike to maintain pressure while staying cloaked. Pulse is right next to me - shadow_strike it, then move away to safety.

Orders say move away from allies and toward map edge. I'm at (5,2), map edge is east at x=5. Moving to (5,5) gets me to the north edge and away from allies clustered around (2,0). First move toward corner.

Pulse (medic) is at (5,5), distance 2 — within attack range but not adjacent. Moving to (5,4) puts me adjacent to Pulse, enabling Denial to block their abilities, then I can attack for 1 damage. Alternatively, I can attack Pulse from current position (dist=2, rng=2) then move. Let me attack Pulse first since they're in range at dist=2, then move to (5,4) to apply Denial pressure and block their patch/abilities next turn. Trap at (3,3) already placed. Bastion is far at dist=6, not a threat this turn.
All enemies are out of range. Closest is Bulwark at dist 3. Move to (4,0) to close gap maximally, then shield_wall facing N to cover the enemy cluster at (4,1)-(4,2) above.

Bastion is adjacent at (4,0). I'm fortified with 50% DR. Orders say attack when possible and shield_wall facing enemy cluster. I'll attack Bastion first, then shield_wall facing South to protect allies from Bastion's direction — wait, Bastion is to my South (4,0) is below me at (4,1). So shield_wall facing S would block damage from Bastion to adjacent allies. Wraith is at (4,2) which is adjacent to me to the North. Shield_wall facing S protects adjacent allies from damage coming from S direction. That helps Wraith if Bastion attacks through me. Attack Bastion then shield_wall S.

Grid (vector) is adjacent at (5,4), so my abilities are blocked by DENIAL. Bastion needs heal but is dist=6, unreachable this turn. I can only move or basic attack. I should move away from Grid to escape denial zone, ideally toward Bastion. Best move toward Bastion while escaping Grid: move to (4,3) reduces dist to Bastion and gets me out of denial range.

Pulse is adjacent at (4,3) and I'm at (4,2). Pulse is healthy but I'm at 2HP. Shadow_strike deals 1dmg and keeps cloak — but I'm not cloaked. I can use regular attack on Pulse since they're adjacent. Or I can move to (4,4) to get behind Pulse, then shadow_strike or attack. Moving to (4,4) puts me behind Pulse (facing S, behind is north of them = (4,4)... wait, behind a S-facing enemy means coming from behind = north side = (4,4) is north of (4,3)? No — Pulse faces S, so their back is to the N, meaning (4,4) is in front and (4,2) is behind. Wait: facing S means looking south, so their back is north. Behind tiles = [4,4]? The data says behindTiles=[[4,4]] which is north. So (4,4) is behind Pulse. I can move to (4,4) first, then shadow_strike Pulse from behind for 1dmg. But I'm not cloaked so shadow_strike won't keep cloak (I'm already visible). Actually shadow_strike just deals 1dmg and keeps cloak if cloaked — I can still use it uncloaked, it's just a 1dmg melee attack. Regular attack also does 1dmg. Either way, 1 damage to Pulse. Better to just attack Pulse now from current position (adjacent), then decide second action. Attack Pulse from (4,2), then move to safety or attack Bastion. Actually Bastion is wounded — priority target. After attacking Pulse, I can't reach Bastion in one move (dist=2). Let me attack Pulse first (they're adjacent, healthy, and a medic — dangerous to leave). Then move toward Bastion or stay.

Bulwark is adjacent at (4,1) - attack immediately. I'm fortified so I don't want to move first and lose 50% damage reduction, but attacking is the priority. Attack Bulwark, then use shield_wall facing North to block damage from the enemy cluster above.

Bastion is adjacent at (4,0) and wounded. I'm fortified with 50% damage reduction. Orders say attack when possible and push aggressively. Best play: attack Bastion first, then shield_wall facing south to protect against Pulse. Staying in place preserves fortify for next turn if I don't move.

Pulse (medic) is adjacent at (4,3), wounded. I can attack or pulse. Moving to (4,4) puts me adjacent to Pulse for a kill next turn, but I'm already at range 2. Pulse ability hits ALL within 1 tile - if I move to (4,4) I'd be at dist 1 from Pulse and could pulse for 1dmg, but also hits allies. Better to just attack Pulse directly (range 2, but attack is adjacent only - dist 2 means not adjacent). Wait - attack is adjacent only (range:adjacent). Pulse ability hits all within 1 tile. So from (5,4), Pulse at (4,3) is distance 2 - not adjacent, can't attack. Can I move closer? Move to (4,4) puts me at dist 1 from Pulse (4,3) - adjacent. Then use attack for 1dmg on wounded Pulse. Alternatively place a trap between enemies. Trap at (3,3) already exists. Place trap at chokepoint to pressure Bastion path. But priority is killing wounded Pulse - move to (4,4) then attack Pulse.
Bastion is wounded and I can reach (3,0) which is behind them and adjacent. Shadow_strike preserves cloak and deals damage. Move to (3,0) then shadow_strike Bastion at (4,0).

I'm adjacent to Grid (vector) at (4,4) which means my abilities are BLOCKED by denial zone - I can only move or basic attack. Bastion is wounded at (4,0) with 3HP needing a heal, but I only have 1 patch use left. I need to move away from Grid's denial zone to use patch on Bastion. Moving to (4,2) gets me closer to Bastion and out of Grid's adjacency. From (4,2) I'm still not adjacent to Bastion (dist=2), but I need to escape denial first. Let me move to (3,2) to break adjacency with Grid and get closer to Bastion.

Bastion is adjacent at (4,0) and wounded — perfect target for attack. I'm fortified so staying put preserves 50% damage reduction. Attack Bastion to finish it off, then use shield_wall facing south to protect against Pulse at (3,2).

Pulse is at (3,2) wounded and dist 3 - I need to close in. Moving to (3,3) would step on my own trap. Move to (4,3) to get closer to Pulse and within range next turn. Then place a trap at (3,3) is already trapped - place at (3,2) area or between enemies. Actually trap is at (3,3) already. Move to (3,4) to approach Pulse at (3,2), then trap at (3,3) is occupied. From (3,4) I can place trap at (3,3) - wait that's occupied. Place trap at (4,3) or (2,4) as a chokepoint. Let me move to (4,3) to be adjacent to Pulse next turn, then trap (3,3) - but trap already there. Trap at (3,2) would hit Pulse if they move - no, trap goes on empty tile. Pulse is at (3,2). Trap at (2,3) or (3,1) as chokepoint between Pulse and my allies at (4,1) and (3,0). Move to (3,4), then trap at (3,3) - occupied. Move to (4,3) then trap at (3,3) - occupied. Move to (3,4) and trap at (2,4) as alternate chokepoint. Actually best: move toward Pulse to get in range - move to (3,4), then trap at (3,3) is occupied so trap at (2,4) or (4,3).

I'm at 2/9 HP, fortified. Two enemies adjacent: Bulwark at (4,1) and Wraith at (3,0). Orders say attack when possible. With only 2HP I need to be careful but orders say push aggressively. I should attack one enemy and use shield_wall to reduce incoming damage. Attacking Bulwark (sentinel) is priority since it's healthy and directly threatening. Shield_wall facing S would block Wraith at (3,0) since Wraith is to my west/south... actually Wraith is at (3,0) which is west of me, and Bulwark is at (4,1) which is north. I'll attack Bulwark (north, dist=1) and shield_wall facing W to block Wraith. Wait - shield_wall range is adjacent ally, not self protection. I should just attack and wait to maintain fortify bonus. If I don't move, I keep 50% damage reduction. Attack Wraith (specter) since specters are high priority threats, then shield_wall to protect Pulse who is wounded.

Bastion is at 2/9 HP and needs healing urgently, but is 3 tiles away at (4,0). I need to move closer first. Moving to (4,2) gets me adjacent to Bastion at (4,0)? No, dist from (4,2) to (4,0) is 2 — not adjacent. Moving to (4,1) is occupied by Bulwark. I can move to (5,1) which is dist 1 from (4,0)? No, (5,1) to (4,0) is dist 2. Actually adjacent means dist=1. From (4,2): Bastion at (4,0) is dist 2. From (5,1): Bastion at (4,0) is dist 2. None of my valid moves put me adjacent to Bastion. Closest I can get is dist 2. But wait — Wraith is wounded at (3,0), dist 2. I can't heal either without adjacency. Bastion is most wounded (2/9). Let me move toward Bastion as best I can. (4,2) gets me dist 2 from Bastion. Also need to avoid Grid at (3,4) — moving to (4,2) keeps me 2 away from Grid, fine. Then I can attack Wraith? No, attack is melee (adjacent). I'll move to (4,2) to position closer to Bastion, then wait — I can't heal from here anyway.

I'm at (3,0) with 1HP, Bastion is adjacent at (4,0) critical and I'm already behind them. Shadow_strike deals 1dmg and keeps cloak. A critical enemy likely has 1HP left — shadow_strike should finish them. Then I can move toward Pulse for next round.

Bastion is critical at (4,0) adjacent south - finishing them is top priority. Pulse is wounded at (4,2) adjacent north. I'm fortified so I have 50% damage reduction. Attack Bastion to eliminate the critical enemy first, then attack Pulse. Both are in range from current position so no need to move.

Pulse is at (4,2), dist 3 - out of range. I have traps at (3,3) and (2,4). I should advance toward Pulse to get in range while placing traps to protect allies. Moving to (4,3) puts me dist 1 from Pulse and enables pulse AoE or attack. But trap is blocked if adjacent to enemy - need to check. Move to (4,3) first, then I'd be adjacent to Pulse at (4,2), so trap would be blocked. Better: move to (3,3) is occupied by trap. Move to (4,4) gets me closer (dist 2 from Pulse at 4,2), then trap at (4,3) to block Pulse's path. That creates a trap between Pulse and my allies.

Wraith(specter) is wounded and adjacent at (3,2). I have 1 patch remaining. Bulwark is adjacent at (4,1) but Grid(vector) is at (4,4) — 2 tiles away, not adjacent, so abilities are not blocked. I should patch Wraith to heal them, then move away from Bulwark if possible.

I'm at 1HP so I need to be careful. Pulse is 2 tiles away at (4,3) facing N, so (4,2) is directly behind them and adjacent. I can move to (4,2) then shadow_strike - shadow_strike keeps cloak and only does 1dmg but Pulse is wounded so this might finish them. Move to (4,2) first, then shadow_strike on Pulse.

Wraith is wounded at (4,2) and Pulse is at (4,3) — Pulse will likely attack Wraith. I need to push north to attack Pulse. Moving to (4,2) would conflict with Wraith's position. I can move to (3,2) or (5,2) to get closer, then shield_wall north to protect Wraith from Pulse. Best: move to (5,2) then shield_wall N to block Pulse's attacks on Wraith at (4,2)... but shield_wall protects adjacent allies from a direction. Wraith at (4,2) is not adjacent to (5,2). Better: stay put and shield_wall N — Wraith at (4,2) is adjacent to my current (4,1)? No, dist=1, yes adjacent. Shield_wall N from (4,1) would block attacks coming from north on adjacent ally Wraith at (4,2). This protects Wraith while I stay fortified.

Pulse medic is adjacent at (4,3) and critical - one hit will finish them. Use attack first to eliminate the medic, then place a trap to control the area since we're now free from adjacent enemy Vector denial... wait, I'm the Vector. Medic is adjacent so I can attack for 1dmg to finish them (critical = low HP). After killing medic, move to better position or place trap. Attack the medic now.

Pulse is adjacent at (4,3), critical health, and I'm already behind them. Shadow_strike deals 1 damage and keeps cloak — perfect to finish them off at 1HP critical. I'm at 1HP so I need to stay cloaked. Use shadow_strike to eliminate Pulse.

I'm at 1/12 HP — critically low. Wraith is wounded and adjacent. I have 1 patch left. I should heal Wraith to keep them in the fight. Grid is 2 tiles away so no ability block. After healing, I need to move away from danger — Bulwark is at (4,1) and Wraith is at (4,2), staying at (4,3) keeps me near enemies. Move to (3,4) to get distance while staying near allies.
Pulse is critical at (3,4), distance 2 - in attack range. Attack to finish them off. Then move to maintain pressure or set trap. Attack first since Pulse is critical and needs to be eliminated.

Pulse is at (3,4), distance 4 - out of attack range. Wraith is wounded at (4,2) adjacent to me. Orders say push forward aggressively. I should advance toward Pulse. Moving to (4,3) gets me closer and maintains pressure. From there I can't attack but I can shield_wall to protect Wraith facing N where Pulse is. Traps at (3,3) and (2,4) - avoid those.
